name: Realm
description: Realm is an offline-first mobile object database that runs directly inside phones, tablets, wearables, and desktop apps. It stores typed objects with automatic indexing and provides real-time reactive queries that auto-update when data changes. Supporting Swift, Kotlin, Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, C#, and .NET, Realm offers zero-copy reads, ACID transactions, and schema migration with minimal boilerplate. It includes an optional device sync service for multi-device data coordination, making it a complete replacement for SQLite and Core Data in mobile applications.
format_on_disk: B+ Tree memory-mapped files (single-file per Realm, optional encryption)
core_strengths: Real-time reactive queries with live objects that auto-update on data changes
Zero-copy reads via memory-mapped storage — no deserialization overhead
ACID transactions with schema migration support and low boilerplate
Offline-first design: all data lives locally, sync is optional and additive
Cross-platform: iOS, Android, .NET, JavaScript/RN, with a unified data model
Single-file storage with built-in encryption, compaction, and notifications
best_use_cases: Mobile apps needing offline-first local persistence with seamless UI updates
React Native apps requiring native-speed object storage with live queries
Cross-platform apps sharing a unified data layer across iOS, Android, and .NET
Replacing SQLite and Core Data with a simpler object-based mobile API
Apps that need optional cloud sync without rearchitecting the local data layer
remarks: Realm is a multi-repo project: realm/realm-swift (iOS/macOS), realm/realm-kotlin (Android), realm/realm-js (JavaScript/TypeScript/React Native), realm/realm-dotnet (.NET). MongoDB acquired Realm in 2019 and announced deprecation of the Atlas Device Sync + Realm SDKs in September 2024. The core database remains open-source (Apache-2.0) and continues to function for local-only use cases, but official maintenance has slowed. The npm package (realm@20.x) requires native module compilation and works with Node.js >= 18 and React Native. Realm objects are live — modifying a queried object automatically updates all references without re-fetching.
